operationalization: Binary feature; test group_ride x platform_type and group_ride x high_unlock_fee x platform_type
rationale: Strong current variation: 8413/14514 rows; official Lime docs describe group rides as unlocking up to five vehicles from one account, so it captures social/group-use affordance.

operationalization: Binary feature currently too common; test cautiously and prefer richer external pass coding: free unlock pass, minute bundle, monthly subscription, first-free-minutes
rationale: Passes alter the effective unlock fee and marginal price. Current field has low variation (13749/14514 rows = 94.7% yes), so richer pass intensity coding would be better.

operationalization: Provider-city-date coding of pass availability and pass type: no unlock fee, minute bundle, monthly subscription, daily pass
rationale: Directly aligns with our high-unlock-fee mechanism: passes remove or soften unlock fees. LimePass and Voi pass examples explicitly combine free unlocks with discounted/prepaid minutes.

operationalization: Provider-city-date coding: can reserve vehicle, reservation duration, reservation fee/deposit
rationale: Reservation reduces search friction and uncertainty, a marketing-place convenience feature. Lime allows 10-minute reservations, extended to 30 minutes for LimePrime/LimePass users.

current_data_fields: Coverage; Exclusive_Coverage; min_parkgebuehr_offroad; mean_parkgebuehr_offroad; parking garage fees
operationalization: Add provider/city parking friction where possible: mandatory stations, flex-zone restrictions, out-of-zone return fees, designated parking requirements
rationale: Return friction can moderate platform/pricing effects: users may avoid services with harder trip-ending rules. nextbike pages discuss official stations/flex zones and reservation deposits; Lime docs mention designated parking in some locations.

operationalization: Binary feature; test complementary_services x platform_type and high_unlock_fee x complementary_services
rationale: Current field is sparse but meaningful (1224 rows). It captures whether providers offer additional service affordances beyond the core ride.

operationalization: Use no_of_vehicle_types_z or dummies for 1/2/3; test x platform_type and x high_unlock_fee
rationale: Product assortment is a classic marketing-mix lever. Current data has 1, 2, or 3 vehicle types, though most rows are single-type.

feature: Public-transport tariff integration / free minutes for subscribers
operationalization: Code city/operator periods where PT subscribers receive free minutes or discounts
rationale: Our promo diagnostics show subscriber-free-minutes is promising but localized. It should be expanded using city/operator campaign data if available.

operationalization: Provider-country/month app ratings, review volume, app version shocks/outages
rationale: Platform membership is mediated through app interfaces; app quality could moderate conversion from platform exposure to utilisation.

operationalization: Provider-city-date coding for referral credits, new-user vouchers, app-store promo copy or campaign pages
rationale: Promotional credits are common in micromobility and directly reduce initial trial friction, but current panel only includes three highly localized promotion types.

operationalization: GIS share of service area under slow/no-ride/mandatory-parking zones; provider-city-date if available
rationale: Geofence restrictions affect realised utility after discovery. Voi public guidance describes no-ride and slow-speed zones visible in app.

operationalization: Extend with lagged availability, vehicle density per service area, stockout proxy, morning fleet presence
rationale: Literature consistently treats availability/accessibility, built environment, weather, transit and temporal factors as key demand determinants.

operationalization: Test marketing-mix x bad-weather interactions by mode and platform type
rationale: E-scooter and bikeshare literature consistently finds weather affects demand and usage patterns; we already have weather fields.

current_data_fields: PT_availability; stations_inhabitant; population_density; ADFC; parking fees
operationalization: Use as moderators for pricing/place features, not core marketing mix
rationale: Reviews identify weather, built environment, public transport, socio-demographics, temporal factors and safety as recurring determinants of shared-bike demand.
